healthSum.title: What is sum-insured health insurance? Do you need it in 2026?
healthSum.metaDesc: What is sum-insured health insurance, how it differs from itemized plans, and whether it’s worth it in 2026, with real cost examples and tips before you decide.
healthSum.s1Body: Sum-insured health insurance is a plan with an "annual total coverage limit" instead of separate limits for room, surgery, and medicine. Example: 5,000,000 THB per year can be used for any treatment within the policy terms.
healthSum.s2Title: How is it different from itemized plans?
healthSum.s2Body: Itemized: e.g. room 3,000 THB/day, surgery 100,000 THB, with a cap per category. Sum-insured: you use one total limit more flexibly and don’t worry if one category runs out.
healthSum.s3Body: In short, "more necessary." Reasons: 1) Medical costs rise every year 2) Critical illness rates are rising 3) Six-figure bills at private hospitals are normal. Examples: appendectomy 80,000–150,000 THB, cancer can run into millions.
healthSum.s4Body: Salaried employees with debt, business owners who can’t afford to stop earning, families, and anyone who wants long-term security.
healthSum.s5Title: What budget should you plan for?
healthSum.s5Body: Generally 1,500–4,000 THB per month, depending on age and coverage amount.
healthSum.s6Body: If you have large savings and can take the risk, it may not be essential. But if a six-figure medical bill would affect your life, sum-insured health insurance is a key risk-management tool.
healthSum.s7Body: If you want to see if it’s right for you, you can get free advice at no cost.

highConverting.title: High-converting landing page for AIA insurance
highConverting.metaDesc: Landing page structure designed to convert leads and build trust for insurance campaigns.
highConverting.s1Body: Headline: Insurance that "fits your life" — not just easy to sell. Subheadline: Free quote in 1 minute, straightforward advice, no pressure. CTA: Get free quote now.
highConverting.s2Body: What if tomorrow you had to pay 200,000 THB for treatment? ICU 30,000–100,000 THB per night, major surgery in the hundreds of thousands to millions, critical illness can stop your income. Many have savings, but savings aren’t meant for "long-term illness." Well-planned insurance protects your savings.
highConverting.s3Title: Shift belief (I don’t sell to everyone)
highConverting.s3Body: What I do: analyze income, assess debt, review health risk, recommend only suitable plans. If it’s not right, I’ll say "you don’t need it yet."
highConverting.s4Body: Case 1: Client 34, emergency hospital stay, bill 187,450 THB, out of pocket 0. Case 2: Business owner, ill and off work 3 months, still received income replacement every month. The difference isn’t luck — it’s planning ahead.
highConverting.s5Title: Offer stack (What you get from free consultation)
highConverting.s5Body: Personal risk analysis, suitable budget calculation, easy plan comparison, no obligation. Takes only 10–15 minutes.
highConverting.s6Body: Get advice within 5 minutes — name, age, job, income, phone, Line ID. FAQ: Must I buy now? No. Really free? Yes. What if I don’t? No obligation.
highConverting.s7Body: No insurance may not be a problem until the day it becomes a big one. Don’t wait for events to force your decision — CTA: Get free quote now.
